# Deep Dive: Reviews and Testimonials

> **Research boundary:** This material is for analysis, ethics, detection, and academic writing. It is not an operational guide for deceptive growth or platform manipulation.

## Core idea

Reviews are compressed trust. They help consumers substitute others’ experience for their own uncertainty. That makes them a prime target for manufactured traction.

## Major review manipulation forms

| Form | Description | Corrupted signal |
|---|---|---|
| Fake review | Reviewer did not use product or does not exist | Authentic user experience |
| Incentivized sentiment | Reward depends on positive/negative tone | Independent judgment |
| Insider review | Employee/family/agent reviews without disclosure | Independence |
| Edited testimonial | Review is trimmed to remove caveats | Message fidelity |
| Review gating | Happy users routed public; unhappy users diverted | Representativeness |
| Suppression | Negative reviews hidden, threatened, or discouraged | Completeness |
| Company-controlled review site | Owned site presented as independent | Source independence |
| Fake influence indicator | Bot views/followers/fake popularity | Public importance |

## Why this matters

Consumers rarely audit every claim. Review systems are decision infrastructure. Fake or filtered reviews therefore damage both consumers and honest competitors.

## Good writing sentence

Fake reviews are not merely fake opinions; they are synthetic market evidence.
